One key point worth emphasizing is that ketamine has well-documented proconvulsant properties, particularly via NMDAR activation, which can precipitate seizures even in individuals prone to epilepsy. Additionally, there may be indirect neurochemical changes induced by ketamine that also contribute to increased seizure likelihood postoperatively.
